title: FACT SHEET: Treasury Department Releases First-Of-Its-Kind Report on Benefits of Unions to the U.S. Economy
summary: The Treasury Department released a comprehensive report by the Office of Economic Policy examining the role of labor unions in the American economy. The report finds that unions raise wages of their members by 10 to 15 percent and contribute to broader economic resilience. Published on August 28, 2023, this fact sheet is a historical policy document from the Biden-Harris Administration advocating for union rights. The source is not directly relevant to financial market structure or macro catalysts for market participants—it's a labor policy advocacy document with no implications for trading, market operations, or traditional macro drivers like rate decisions, inflation data, or economic releases.
key_points: Unions raise wages of their members by 10 to 15 percent according to the Treasury report
Union workers value ability to avoid short-notice schedule changes at up to 20 percent of wages
Union representation rate for Black men was 13 percent in 2021, compared to 10 percent population average
Union election petitions in 2022 reached their highest level since 2015
Executive Order 14063 requires project labor agreements on federal construction projects of $35 million or more
New rule updating Davis-Bacon prevailing wage regulations affects over one million construction workers
why_it_matters: This source is not relevant for financial market research or trading purposes. It is a labor policy fact sheet summarizing the Biden-Harris Administration's advocacy for unions. It contains no market-moving data, no commentary on monetary policy, and no analysis of financial markets or macro catalysts that would affect asset prices.
important_numbers: $35 million threshold for project labor agreement requirements
